Title: The plasma membrane aquaporin ZmPIP2;5 enhances the sensitivity of stomatal closure to water deficit

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The pip2;5 KD line is a knockdown, not a null mutant; a low level of PIP2;5 expression is still detectable, and Western blot shows a 36% reduction in epidermal PIP2;5 protein relative to WT-W22. PIP2;5 expression in leaves is lower than in roots. The functional phenotypes (transpiration, gs, stomatal aperture, ROS) are strongly condition-dependent, being most evident under mild water deficit and low ABA concentrations, but not under severe water deficit or high ABA, suggesting different ABA signalling contributions from guard cells versus vascular bundle sheath cells.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PIP2;5 is a plasma membrane aquaporin of maize expressed in roots and leaves, with detectable protein in epidermal and subsidiary cells of stomatal complexes and lower abundance in guard cells; overexpressing lines show about twofold higher epidermal PIP2;5 signal, while the knockdown line shows reduced signal (~36% reduction) compared with WT.
2. PIP2;5 overexpression increases whole-plant transpiration under well-watered and severe water deficit conditions, whereas under mild water deficit (ψ_soil = -0.1 to -0.2 MPa) and high VPD (~2.2–2.5 kPa) it promotes a stronger decrease in transpiration compared with WT; conversely, pip2;5 knockdown plants show higher transpiration than WT under mild water deficit.
3. In excised leaves, low ABA concentration (0.1 μM) induces a faster and larger decrease in stomatal conductance in PIP2;5 OE lines than in WT, whereas pip2;5 KD leaves show an attenuated decrease; these differences disappear at higher ABA concentrations (≥0.5 μM).
4. In epidermal peels treated with 10 μM ABA, stomata of PIP2;5 OE lines close significantly within 30 min, while WT stomata require 60 min to show a significant reduction in aperture; in contrast, pip2;5 KD stomata are insensitive or even show slightly increased aperture in response to ABA.
5. ABA-induced H2O2 accumulation in guard cells is accelerated and enhanced in PIP2;5 OE lines (significant increase after 2 min of 10 μM ABA), delayed in WT (significant only after 15 min), and abolished in pip2;5 KD plants, indicating that PIP2;5 is required for rapid ABA-dependent ROS build-up in guard cells.
6. These results support a model in which PIP2;5 acts as an H2O2 and water channel in guard-cell and subsidiary-cell plasma membranes, enhancing ABA signalling sensitivity and hydraulic responses, thereby increasing the sensitivity and speed of stomatal closure and transpiration decrease under mild water deficit and high VPD, which can be beneficial for water conservation and growth under moderate drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIP2;5 increases the sensitivity and speed of ABA- and mild water deficit-induced stomatal closure and enhances the decrease in transpiration under mild drought and high vapour pressure deficit; its downregulation reduces ABA sensitivity and slows or abolishes stomatal closure and ROS accumulation in guard cells.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Upon mild water deficit or low ABA, PIP2;5 in guard-cell and subsidiary-cell plasma membranes facilitates rapid H2O2 influx into guard cells, leading to earlier and stronger ROS accumulation, which promotes activation of guard-cell ion transport systems, outward water flow, turgor loss and stomatal closure; under high VPD, active PIP2;5 water channels accelerate guard-cell water efflux, causing faster turgor decrease and enhanced sensitivity of transpiration decrease.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA-dependent stomatal closure and hydraulic responses to water deficit and high vapour pressure deficit, in which ABA signalling and apoplastic H2O2 production in guard cells drive ROS accumulation, activation of membrane transport systems, loss of turgor and stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plasma membrane intrinsic protein aquaporin, PIP2 subfamily
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane of epidermal and subsidiary cells in stomatal complexes; low abundance in guard cells
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in maize root tissues at higher levels than in leaves, and detectable in mature leaf zone, epidermis and stomatal complexes, with strong signal in subsidiary and epidermal cells and weak signal in guard cells.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil water status: well-watered soil; mild water deficit with ψ_soil = -0.1 to -0.2 MPa; severe water deficit with ψ_soil ≈ -0.3 to -0.6 MPa, imposed by withholding irrigation. Vapour pressure deficit: low VPD ~1 kPa vs high VPD ~2.2–2.5 kPa in growth chambers. ABA treatments: 0.1 μM ABA applied via the transpiration stream to excised leaves for gs measurements; 10 μM ABA applied to epidermal peels for stomatal aperture and ROS measurements; higher ABA concentrations (≥0.5 μM) tested for gs but did not show PIP2;5-dependent differences.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of stomatal closure dynamics, transpiration and water relations by a maize aquaporin under water deficit, ABA treatment and high vapour pressure deficit, with implications for drought resilience and water use efficiency.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress responses (mild and severe drought / soil water deficit, high vapour pressure deficit) affecting stomatal movement, stomatal conductance, transpiration and water-use traits.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Immunolocalization and Western blot: PIP2;5 fluorescence signal in stomatal complexes is roughly twofold higher in both OE lines than in WT-B104 and about twofold lower in pip2;5 KD than in WT-W22; Western blot quantification shows a 36% decrease of PIP2;5 signal in pip2;5 KD epidermis compared with WT-W22. Transpiration: in well-watered soil, PIP2;5 OE-4 and OE-13 plants transpire 22% and 14% more than WT-B104, respectively, while pip2;5 KD plants show similar transpiration to WT-W22; under mild water deficit (ψ_soil = -0.1 to -0.2 MPa), OE-4 and OE-13 plants transpire 16% and 9% less than WT-B104, whereas pip2;5 KD plants transpire 31% more than WT-W22. Under severe water deficit (ψ_soil ≈ -0.3 to -0.5 MPa), OE plants transpire more than WT. Stomatal conductance: after 0.1 μM ABA addition to excised leaves, gs decreases faster and to a larger extent in both OE lines than in WT, while pip2;5 KD leaves show a smaller gs decrease; this difference disappears at 0.5 μM ABA. Stomatal aperture: in epidermal peels treated with 10 μM ABA, OE stomata show a significant reduction in aperture already after 30 min, whereas WT stomata show a significant decrease only after 60 min; in pip2;5 KD, ABA treatment slightly increases stomatal aperture relative to control. ROS: in OE guard cells, H2O2-dependent fluorescence increases significantly after 2 min of 10 μM ABA, while WT shows significant increase only after 15 min; pip2;5 KD guard cells do not show detectable ROS accumulation at any ABA incubation time, whereas WT-W22 siblings show increased ROS after 15 min ABA.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In well-watered conditions, PIP2;5 overexpression increases daily transpiration by about 14–22% relative to WT, while pip2;5 knockdown decreases daily transpiration by about 9% or shows similar rates depending on conditions. Under mild soil water deficit (ψ_soil = -0.1 to -0.2 MPa), overexpressors transpire 9–16% less than WT, whereas the pip2;5 knockdown plants transpire about 31% more than WT. Under severe soil water deficit (ψ_soil ≈ -0.3 to -0.5 MPa), PIP2;5 OE plants transpire more than WT. Under high VPD (~2.2–2.5 kPa) PIP2;5 OE plants show a stronger decrease in transpiration over time than WT plants, whereas at low VPD (~1 kPa) OE plants initially transpire more than WT.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic maize lines overexpressing PIP2;5 (PIP2;5 OE-4, PIP2;5 OE-13) in the B104 background and a pip2;5 knockdown line generated by a Mu transposon insertion in the W22 background, together with WT-B104 and WT-W22 siblings. Leaves (intact plants, excised leaves) and abaxial epidermal peels from 2–3-week-old plants grown in soil in growth chambers or greenhouse.
Plant_Genes.Variety: B104 and W22 inbred lines (WT-B104, WT-W22 backgrounds for PIP2;5 OE and pip2;5 KD lines)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: In situ immunolocalization of PIP2;5 and PMA in peeled epidermis with confocal microscopy; Western blot of epidermal protein extracts; continuous gravimetric measurements of whole-plant transpiration under controlled soil water potentials and vapour pressure deficits (Phenodyn platform); Li-Cor 6400XT gas exchange measurements of stomatal conductance in excised leaves with ABA treatments; stomatal aperture measurements in epidermal peels incubated with ABA; ROS detection in guard cells by H2DCFDA staining after ABA treatment; statistical analyses by Student's t test and one-way ANOVA with Tukey's posttest.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously characterized plasma membrane aquaporin; this study uses reverse genetics with PIP2;5 overexpressing (OE-4, OE-13) and Mu-transposon knockdown (pip2;5 KD) maize lines described earlier, and compares them with their WT siblings to assess function.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PIP2;5 is a promising target to improve maize drought resilience and water use efficiency by modifying stomatal closure dynamics. Overexpression increases the sensitivity and speed of stomatal closure to mild soil water deficit and high VPD, enhancing early transpiration decrease, water conservation and maintenance of leaf growth under moderate stress. However, because overexpression also increases transpiration under well-watered and severe drought conditions, optimal manipulation (e.g., tissue- or condition-specific expression) would be needed to exploit PIP2;5 for breeding water-efficient, drought-resilient maize cultivars.
